[2018-04-02 23:47] .bky_1556 (bky): I like Crystal Forge. Unfortunately, it falls afoul of the click cycle rule,
[2018-04-02 23:48] .bky_1556 (bky): a relatively obscure design rule in the game where you can't have one click partly undo another click's resource conversions.
[2018-04-02 23:49] .bky_1556 (bky): So there should be no situation where you click this to turn B (and stuff) into G and then use Synthesizer to turn G back into B.
[2018-04-02 23:51] .bky_1556 (bky): This only applies to resources, not units.
[2018-04-02 23:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): yes it's about order of operations so you can't undo shit to get into better positions
[2018-04-02 23:59] .bky_1556 (bky): It's a different rule from SDR, yes.
[2018-04-03 00:00] .bky_1556 (bky): We should make a Reddit article listing all these design standards.

[2018-04-04 03:27] kaelii: Trying to figure out on a very simple unit what kind of upfront discount/mercenary payments and effects are reasonable.
